title: dalea leporina var. alba
intro: Dalea leporina var. alba, also known as White Prairie Clover, is a perennial legume native to the Great Plains region of North America, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and narrow leaflets. The medicinal properties of White Prairie Clover include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. White Prairie Clover can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or powder, and is often used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
